![VINUNI INTERNATIONAL STUDENTS ASSOCIATION (VISA)](https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/wp-content/uploads/sites/20/2025/05/474982531_622237893497905_6054611251967269999_n.jpg)

**1. Club Introduction**

The VinUni International Students Association (VISA) serves as a platform for cultural exchange, providing opportunities for students from diverse backgrounds to connect, learn, and celebrate their differences. VISA advocates for the rights and interests of incoming and current international students, offering support and resources to navigate challenges and promote a welcoming environment on campus.

**2. Highlight events**

Through student-led initiatives and events, VISA plays pivotal role in fostering a sense of community and inclusivity among international students to facilitate a smoother integration into their lives in Vietnam. Some Key Events include: Culture cuisines and festival nights, International Book and Culture Exhibition.

**3. For more information please visit**

Feel free to reach out to us if you have any questions about international student life at VinUni.

Email: [visavinuniversity@gmail.com](mailto:visavinuniversity@gmail.com)  
Fanpage: [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/visa.assoc) ; [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/visa.assoc/)

## Page: https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/new-students/oasis-program

OASIS is a distinctive signature program of VinUniversity that holds a registered trademark. Its primary objective is to cultivate a self-leadership mindset and equip students with essential skills, facilitating their transformation from high school students into effective university scholars and responsible global citizens who are ready for future careers. OASIS is implemented in the first 4 years of a bachelor’s degree to ensure enough time for students to reflect and practice their self-leadership development, especially in developing essential qualities for work and life, including self-confidence, self-esteem, self-determination, and self-control.

## Page: https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/new-students/oasis-program/o-orientation

This block aims to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of the nature, value, and requirements of a university education.
Freshmen are required to participate in the Week of Welcome. All students are obligated to annually take the Code of Conduct and Academic Integrity Test, with assessment results graded on a pass/fail basis. The tests will be conducted on Canvas and you will be added to the course to complete at the beginning of every academic year.

## Page: https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/new-students/oasis-program/a-advising

The objective of this block is to assist students in successfully navigating their university learning journey, addressing both academic and career matters. No formal assessment is required but submission of IDP is mandatory.

## Page: https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/new-students/oasis-program/s-skill

This block offers additional benefits to VinUni students, providing them with a self-learning platform and a series of structured micro-learning skills workshops, organized on-demand to prepare them for college and career readiness. Participation at Career Readiness Checkpoint (CRC) is mandatory.

## Page: https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/new-students/oasis-program/i-identity-culture

Building an identity for VinUni Students based on the core values of VinUni. VinUni students embody empathy, exceptional capability, creativity, an entrepreneurial spirit, and leadership mindset throughout their journey from the moment they enter the university, through interactive courses and extracurricular activities.

## Page: https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/new-students/oasis-program/s-spirit-of-pay-it-forward

This component represents a unique experiential learning opportunity that integrates students’ academic studies with community-based projects, fostering a sense of responsibility as global citizens committed to unpaid volunteer work and the “pay it forward” spirit. Every student is expected to complete 45 hours of community service volunteering throughout their academic journey. These projects can be initiated by VinUni students, the Lead Faculty of Community Service Learning (CSL), or by any individuals and organizations within or beyond VinUni.

# Tuition Rates and Fees

Undergraduate 

Postgraduate 

Living Expenses

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Program** | **Standard Duration (years)** | **Listed Tuition Fee per Academic Year** | **Listed Tuition Fee per Semester** | **Listed Tuition Fee per Credit (\*)** |
| Bachelor of Nursing | 4 | 349,650,000/year | 174,825,000/semester | 9,780,000/credit |
| Doctor of Medicine | 6 | 815,850,000/year | 407,925,000/ semester | 27,195,000/ credit |
| Other Bachelor Programs | 4 | 815,850,000/year | 407,925,000/ semester | 27,195,000/ credit |

(\*) Tuition fees per credit apply for:

* Double degree students at VinUniversity.
* Integrated exchange programs with partners.
* Short-term exchanges requiring credit payment.
* Transferring students, evaluated per VinUniversity regulations. Conditional credit transfers requiring extra coursework will incur fees based on those credits, applicable to the 2024-2025 cohort.

*Note: 15 class hours equal 1 credit.*

Tuition fees are the same for all students, regardless of learning method, and are paid twice a year at the start of each semester.

## Page: https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/student-life-support

The groundbreaking ceremony on 14 November 2018 marked the first step towards building the physical foundation for the VinUniversity campus, the home of Vietnam’s world-class VinUniversity. The campus is located in Vinhomes Ocean Park in the Gia Lam District, Hanoi, Vietnam, with a total area of 420 hectares. With only a 19% building density and with 117 hectares dedicated to trees and natural areas, the campus will be an ideal setting for self-directed and collaborative learning, peaceful introspection, and inspirational beauty.
The landscape, architecture, and interior at VinUniversity deliver the optimal learner-centered environment. Lecture halls and classrooms is designed to enable a highly flexible and adaptable active learning environment, suitable for lectures, interactive and collaborative learning models, and evidence-based methods, such as Team-Based Learning (TBL), Project-Based Learning (PBL), and Simulation-Based Learning (SBL).

# Library

![](https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/wp-content/uploads/sites/20/2025/05/Canva-4-microsite.png)

**Library access**

* Students, faculty, and staff who would like to access our library’s physical building and borrow physical materials/equipment must possess a validated VinUni card and are required to present it on request; only users who own a validated VinUni ID can access our library’s subscribed electronic materials.
* VinUni staff/adjunct faculty/affiliated faculty who wish to access library resources, are welcome to apply via the **[VinUni Library ID Registration Form](https://library.vinuni.edu.vn/registration-for-vinuni-library-account/)** on the library website.
* Eligible staff from other Vingroup’s P&Ls or VinUni’s counterparts who wish to access electronic resources from VinUni library, are welcome to apply for a VinUni ID via the **[VinUni Library ID Registration Form](https://library.vinuni.edu.vn/registration-for-vinuni-library-account/)** on the library website.
* Sharing VinUni library ID or VinUni cards with others is strictly prohibited.

[Library Resources](https://library.vinuni.edu.vn/)

## Regulations on the use of library materials

**1. Circulation regulations for library materials**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **No.** | **Material types** | **Circulated or Uncirculated** | **Circulation locations** | **Notes** |
| 1 | General print books | Circulated | * Self-check machines (1st Floor & 2nd Floor) * Circulation desk (1st Floor) * 24/7 book return machine |  |
| 2 | Reference materials and print journals | Uncirculated | * Not applicable |  |
| 3 | Audiovisual materials (CDs/DVDs) | Circulated | * Circulation desk (1st Floor) only |  |
| 4 | Books in Course Reserve collection | Circulated | * Circulation desk (1st Floor) only | These books can be checked out for 02 hours only (01 item/user/time). |

* Patrons MUST return or renew all borrowed materials before its overdue time.
* The renewal period is half of the original loan period. Renewals of library materials are only allowed if there has been no request for that material by others. Materials that are not overdue can be renewed in person at the circulation desk, or online. Overdue materials cannot be renewed.
* Patrons need to inform library staff at the circulation desk about any damage to the materials before check-out/check-in.
* When the due date falls on a holiday or when the library is closed, the due date is adjusted to the following day when the library is open.
* Patrons are prohibited to misfile or hide library materials for personal use or bring physical materials out of the library without going through borrowing procedures as regulated.
* All users must return all loans, fines and debts must be paid, one month before their departure days.
* All mercies regarding fines and loan periods need to be approved by university senior leaders as regulated by VinUni’s document of authorization.

**2. Circulation Privileges**

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **User groups** | **Number of items** | **Loan Period** | **Renewals** |
| * Undergraduate students | 3 | 2 weeks | 1 time |
| * Graduate students | 5 | 1 month | 1 time |
| * Affiliated faculty * VinUni’s full time staff / Service-contract staff | 3 | 2 weeks | 1 time |
| * VinUni’s faculty | 5 | 6 months | 1 time |
| * Visiting students | To be determined by library leader. | | |
| * Guests/Interns/Alumni | 0 | 0 | 0 |

*Note: The loan period of 06 months will be applied for faculty who borrow textbooks for course use. However, their loaned items can be subject to recall if the items are requested by other users, and they need to send a request to the library about the textbooks for course use.*

**3. Making physical material request**

* Patrons can make a request via the library website to borrow a physical book or a CD/DVD, etc. that has already been circulated to others. A material request will not be placed if:
* The item is in the course reserve collection.
* The item is lost, missing, or non-circulating.
* Patrons’ borrowing privileges have been blocked or suspended.
* A patron has reached their loan limit (the maximum number of items that they can borrow).
* Library items ready for pickup will be kept for 02 days from the tentative pick-up date. If it is not picked up by the stipulated date, the request will lapse.
* Patrons themselves must personally collect the library materials on hold for them.

**4. Recalls**

All loans are subject to recall after one-week’s usage. If an item is recalled, a Recall Notice will be sent to patrons via email. Patrons are requested to return materials by a new shorter due date.

**5. Electronic Resources Use**

Subscribed electronic resources such as databases, e-journals and e-books are covered by licensing agreements between the VinUni and individual providers. Therefore,

* The use of subscribed electronic resources at the library is limited to VinUni students, faculty, and staff only, unless allowed by the license agreement.
* Patrons accessing library databases will be required to log in with their VinUni ID.
* Patrons must use electronic resources properly; ensuring that materials retrieved from electronic resources are limited to personal and non-commercial purposes. Improper use generally includes, but is not limited to:

**Excessive downloading:**

* Rapid downloading of multiple articles.
* Continuous downloading of tables of contents, search results, or citations.
* Downloading entire issues or volumes of an e-journal.
* Using robots, spiders, or other automated programs to download subscription resources

**Other license violations:**

* Sharing passwords with unauthorized patrons
* Redistributing information from online resources to unauthorized patrons
* Posting information retrieved or downloaded from an online resource to a listserv, on a website, or to an email list
* Reselling, redistributing, or reproducing any journal text, output, search result, or other information in any form or medium
* Redistribution of materials to non-subscribers or non-subscribing institutions.

Unauthorized use of VinUni Library databases and other electronic resources can result in access being revoked for the entire university, and therefore will be disciplined as outlined in Section 4 below, or by referral to appropriate civil authorities.

**6. Material photocopying/ printing/scanning services**

* Photocopying/printing/scanning services are provided for patrons with research, personal study, and non-commercial purposes.
* Patrons must avoid any breach of copyright in using photocopiers, printers, and scanners in the library including but not limited to:
  + Making more than one copy of the work by an author for any purpose without author’s permission.
  + Digitize physical materials and distribute the digital copy to other people with any purposes.
  + Modify, mutilate, or misrepresent the material in any manner which is prejudicial to the honor and reputation of the authors.
  + Create derivative material (the material, which is translated from one language into another adapted, modified, transformed, etc. from other materials) without permission of the authors/ copyright owners of original materials.
  + Intentionally rescind or invalidate technical measures taken by copyright owners to protect copyright in their materials.
  + Deliberately delete or change copyright management information in electronic format contained in the materials.
* Users are fully responsible for any legal consequences concerning copyright that might arise from using the library’s photocopiers, printers, and scanners.

# Research Opportunities

### Student Research at VinUni

Undergraduate Research at VinUniversity is a core part of the training and education of VinUni students. VinUni recognizes that research skills and competencies are not just critical for those who wish to purse academic work, but for any person who wishes to be a contributor, leader and a change maker in the society. Research skills of systematic inquiry, information gathering, critical analysis, problem solving, resilience and focus, interpretation and communication, are core life skills that are part of VinUni’s mission for students!  
First year students are already engaged in independent research under the guidance of VinUni faculty members, and working with faculty on their Seed Grants and other research projects.

[CECS](https://cecs.vinuni.edu.vn/uropcecs/)

## Page: https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/global-exchange

INTEGRATED DEGREE PROGRAMS

At VinUniversity (VinUni), we offer integrated degree programs alongside traditional tracks, allowing students to fast track their studies. These programs, established with renowned partner universities, enable students to earn two bachelor’s degrees or a bachelor’s and a master’s degree.
Students will complete Phase 1 at VinUni and Phase 2 at a partner university abroad. Program formats include 3+2, 4+1, and 2+2 options.
The fast-tracking Bachelor’s–Master’s module lets students earn credits toward their Master’s while completing their Bachelor’s, potentially saving costs and allowing graduation up to 12 months earlier. This pathway promotes a smooth transition to higher degrees efficiently and cost-effectively.

## Page: https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/career-opportunities

VinUniversity Career Services guide students through the R.E.D.I. Roadmap (Reflect, Explore, Develop, Implement) and 5C Model—offering career exploration, career readiness workshops, 1-on-1 career counseling, career mentoring, and career checkpoints (including Career Festival and Career Day). Through the EXCEL framework, students build key skills and gain real-world experience via work-study roles and internships with top companies. Support continues up to two years after graduation. Additional resources include job search tools, the Career Development Guidebook and the Career Advancement Guidebook. VinUniversity works closely with industry partners to bridge academics and employment.

## Page: https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/career-opportunities/__trashed

The Student Work-Study Program at VinUniversity is designed to provide students with opportunities to gain practical work experience, develop valuable skills, and contribute to the university community. This program also aims to support students financially while fostering their professional and personal growth.

## Page: https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/international-student

Our international students are not just part of the VinUni family, they are ambassadors of their cultures and essential contributors to our global dialogue. At VinUniversity, we celebrate this diversity daily, ensuring that every voice is heard and every perspective valued. Together, we’re not just building a university, we’re creating a global community of leaders, thinkers, and innovators.

## Page: https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/international-student/international-student-services

VinUniversity provides dedicated support for international students, including visa guidance, financial advising, transportation support, housing assistance, and diverse dining options. These services ensure a smooth transition and help students feel at home while focusing on their academic and personal growth in Vietnam.

## Page: https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/international-student/vinuniversity-international-students-community

VinUniversity hosts a dynamic international student community from over 16 countries. International students receive a globally competitive education and are actively involved in campus life through co-living, student clubs like VISA, and cultural events such as the Hanoi Tour and Year-End Party.
With strong support services, including visa assistance and the Student Affairs Office, VinUniversity ensures a smooth transition and enriching university experience.

VinUniversity utilizes **Canvas**, a web-based Learning Management System (LMS), for both credit and non-credit courses. Instructors use Canvas to manage course content, assignments, quizzes, schedules, and communication, while students are expected to log in daily to track coursework. Canvas offers interactive features like threaded discussions, video conferencing, and group collaboration tools.  
Before the semester starts, all students must complete the **Passport to Canvas** online course to familiarize themselves with the platform. To enhance accessibility, students are encouraged to download the **Canvas app** on their mobile devices for seamless learning and engagement throughout their academic journey.

### 1. What is elab

Entrepreneurship Lab (E-Lab) is the heart of innovation at VinUniversity. We provide a hands-on, student-led technopreneurial ecosystem where students, alumni, and young entrepreneurs transform ideas into impactful startups through mentorship, funding, and structured programs.

[INTRODUCING VIDEO](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4d1xDyKgqRc&t=5s)

### 2. Why Elab?

Backed by Vingroup – Vietnam’s leading conglomerate  
Global-local network: Cornell, Upenn, FUV, HUST, and more  
100+ C-level mentors and entrepreneurs-in-residence  
Startup-friendly ecosystem: co-working space, free tools, legal support  
Impact: 40+ student startups/year, $3.5M+ raised, 2,000+ community members

### 3. Key Programs and Services

**Pre-Incubator**

Focus: Ideation, early-stage development  
Activities: Peer mentoring, workshops, competitions  
Open to: All students

**Incubator**

Focus: Business model validation  
Benefits: $2,000 grant, mentorship, workspace  
Open to: Teams with at least one VinUnian

**Pre-Accelerator (Venture X)**

Focus: Scaling, investment readiness  
Benefits: $10,000 seed fund, pitch events, global mentors  
Co-hosted with HUB Network

**Venture Studio & Deep Tech Launchpad**

Focus: R&D, emerging tech, market entry  
Ideal for: AI, IoT, sustainability, healthtech startups

### 4. Mentorship Network

**Peer Mentors**: Student founders supporting peers

**Industry Mentors**: Entrepreneurs, investors, and professionals

**Entrepreneurs-in-Residence (EiRs)**: Senior mentors guiding high-potential teams

### 5. Students’ Startups Showcase

**MindPal** – AI agents platform, $360K revenue, 2K+ users

**Finful** – Financial education app, Top awards, partnered with Techcombank

**Dynapath** – Energy tiles from plastic waste, $2.5M valuation

### 6. Events & Competitions

[Global Entreprep Incubator](https://www.facebook.com/share/p/15xQcYKMq1/)  
[Green Industrial AI Challenge](https://www.facebook.com/share/p/1BwvXGNkdj/)

Work-Study Program 2024-2025: Practical Work Opportunities for VinUniversity Students

### 1. Purpose

The Student Work-Study Program at VinUniversity is designed to provide students with opportunities to gain practical work experience, develop valuable skills, and contribute to the university community. This program also aims to support students financially while fostering their professional and personal growth.

### 2. Program details

### 2.1. Work Hours

Students participating in the Work-Study Program are limited to working a maximum of 24 hours per week during the academic year. This restriction ensures that students can balance their academic commitments effectively while gaining work experience and complying to Vietnamese laws.

### 2.2. Pay rates

Pay rates are established according to the skills and responsibilities specified in the job description.

The two standard pay rates are:

* 41,667 VND/hour: For roles that require basic skills or minimal prior experience.
* 83,333 VND/hour: For roles that demand advanced skills or specialized expertise, as outlined in the job description.

### 2.3. Placement Areas:

Students can be placed in various departments, including academic offices, research projects, library services, student affairs, and others, based on the specific needs of each department during the academic year.

### 2.4. Eligibility Criteria:

a) Must be a full-time undergraduate or graduate student.

b) Maintain satisfactory academic progress, as per university regulations.

c) Demonstrate financial need (if applicable) or meet department-specific requirements.

d) Commitment to balancing work responsibilities with academic obligations.

### 2.5. Application Process

**Step 1: Job postings available:**

All available Work-Study positions will be listed on the [Job Portal](http://careerportal.vinuni.edu.vn/).

**Step 2: Application period:**

Students can submit their applications through Job Portal

**Step 3: Application review:**

The Student Affairs Management (SAM) team will collect all applications and forward them to hiring supervisors for review

**Step 4: Interview & selection:**

Colleges and departments will conduct interviews with applicants and finalize the results

**Step 5: Final results & onboarding:**

SAM will send offer letters and provide contract processing details

Successful applicants may have to attend the Onboarding Orientation

Step: Start of work:

The employment contract will be effective and announced by SAM

### 3. Reference

[Student Work-Study Program Guidelines – VinUniversity Policy](https://policy.vinuni.edu.vn/all-policies/work-study-program-guidelines/)

![VINUNI INTERNATIONAL STUDENTS ASSOCIATION (VISA)](https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/wp-content/uploads/sites/20/2025/05/474982531_622237893497905_6054611251967269999_n.jpg)

**1. Club Introduction**

The VinUni International Students Association (VISA) serves as a platform for cultural exchange, providing opportunities for students from diverse backgrounds to connect, learn, and celebrate their differences. VISA advocates for the rights and interests of incoming and current international students, offering support and resources to navigate challenges and promote a welcoming environment on campus.

**2. Highlight events**

Through student-led initiatives and events, VISA plays pivotal role in fostering a sense of community and inclusivity among international students to facilitate a smoother integration into their lives in Vietnam. Some Key Events include: Culture cuisines and festival nights, International Book and Culture Exhibition.

**3. For more information please visit**

Feel free to reach out to us if you have any questions about international student life at VinUni.

Email: [visavinuniversity@gmail.com](mailto:visavinuniversity@gmail.com)  
Fanpage: [Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/visa.assoc) ; [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/visa.assoc/)

Copyright © 2025 VinUni. All Right Reserved.

OASIS is a distinctive signature program of VinUniversity that holds a registered trademark. Its primary objective is to cultivate a self-leadership mindset and equip students with essential skills, facilitating their transformation from high school students into effective university scholars and responsible global citizens who are ready for future careers. OASIS is implemented in the first 4 years of a bachelor’s degree to ensure enough time for students to reflect and practice their self-leadership development, especially in developing essential qualities for work and life, including self-confidence, self-esteem, self-determination, and self-control.

# Handbooks

[![International Student Handbook](https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/wp-content/uploads/sites/20/2025/08/STUDENT-GUIDE-2025-2026-2.png)

### Student Guide](https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/wp-content/uploads/sites/20/2025/08/STUDENT-GUIDE-2025-2026.pdf)

[![International Student Handbook](https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/wp-content/uploads/sites/20/2025/08/Residential-Living-Guidelines.png)

### Residential Living Guidelines](https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/wp-content/uploads/sites/20/2025/08/Residential-LivingGuidelines.pdf)

[![International Student Handbook](https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/wp-content/uploads/sites/20/2025/08/International-Student-Welcome-Guide.png)

### International Student Welcome Guide](https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/wp-content/uploads/sites/20/2025/11/International-Student-Welcome-Guide-1.pdf)

[![logo](https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/wp-content/themes/launch-pad/assets/images/logo_vin.png)](javascript:void(0);)

Copyright © 2025 VinUni. All Right Reserved.

This block aims to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of the nature, value, and requirements of a university education.
Freshmen are required to participate in the Week of Welcome. All students are obligated to annually take the Code of Conduct and Academic Integrity Test, with assessment results graded on a pass/fail basis. The tests will be conducted on Canvas and you will be added to the course to complete at the beginning of every academic year.

The objective of this block is to assist students in successfully navigating their university learning journey, addressing both academic and career matters. No formal assessment is required but submission of IDP is mandatory.

This block offers additional benefits to VinUni students, providing them with a self-learning platform and a series of structured micro-learning skills workshops, organized on-demand to prepare them for college and career readiness. Participation at Career Readiness Checkpoint (CRC) is mandatory.

Building an identity for VinUni Students based on the core values of VinUni. VinUni students embody empathy, exceptional capability, creativity, an entrepreneurial spirit, and leadership mindset throughout their journey from the moment they enter the university, through interactive courses and extracurricular activities.

This component represents a unique experiential learning opportunity that integrates students’ academic studies with community-based projects, fostering a sense of responsibility as global citizens committed to unpaid volunteer work and the “pay it forward” spirit. Every student is expected to complete 45 hours of community service volunteering throughout their academic journey. These projects can be initiated by VinUni students, the Lead Faculty of Community Service Learning (CSL), or by any individuals and organizations within or beyond VinUni.

| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Program** | **Standard Duration (years)** | **Listed Tuition Fee per Academic Year** | **Listed Tuition Fee per Semester** | **Listed Tuition Fee per Credit (\*)** |
| Bachelor of Nursing | 4 | 349,650,000/year | 174,825,000/semester | 9,780,000/credit |
| Doctor of Medicine | 6 | 815,850,000/year | 407,925,000/ semester | 27,195,000/ credit |
| Other Bachelor Programs | 4 | 815,850,000/year | 407,925,000/ semester | 27,195,000/ credit |

(\*) Tuition fees per credit apply for:

* Double degree students at VinUniversity.
* Integrated exchange programs with partners.
* Short-term exchanges requiring credit payment.
* Transferring students, evaluated per VinUniversity regulations. Conditional credit transfers requiring extra coursework will incur fees based on those credits, applicable to the 2024-2025 cohort.

*Note: 15 class hours equal 1 credit.*

Tuition fees are the same for all students, regardless of learning method, and are paid twice a year at the start of each semester.

The groundbreaking ceremony on 14 November 2018 marked the first step towards building the physical foundation for the VinUniversity campus, the home of Vietnam’s world-class VinUniversity. The campus is located in Vinhomes Ocean Park in the Gia Lam District, Hanoi, Vietnam, with a total area of 420 hectares. With only a 19% building density and with 117 hectares dedicated to trees and natural areas, the campus will be an ideal setting for self-directed and collaborative learning, peaceful introspection, and inspirational beauty.
The landscape, architecture, and interior at VinUniversity deliver the optimal learner-centered environment. Lecture halls and classrooms is designed to enable a highly flexible and adaptable active learning environment, suitable for lectures, interactive and collaborative learning models, and evidence-based methods, such as Team-Based Learning (TBL), Project-Based Learning (PBL), and Simulation-Based Learning (SBL).

# Library

![](https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/wp-content/uploads/sites/20/2025/05/Canva-4-microsite.png)

**Library access**

* Students, faculty, and staff who would like to access our library’s physical building and borrow physical materials/equipment must possess a validated VinUni card and are required to present it on request; only users who own a validated VinUni ID can access our library’s subscribed electronic materials.
* VinUni staff/adjunct faculty/affiliated faculty who wish to access library resources, are welcome to apply via the **[VinUni Library ID Registration Form](https://library.vinuni.edu.vn/registration-for-vinuni-library-account/)** on the library website.
* Eligible staff from other Vingroup’s P&Ls or VinUni’s counterparts who wish to access electronic resources from VinUni library, are welcome to apply for a VinUni ID via the **[VinUni Library ID Registration Form](https://library.vinuni.edu.vn/registration-for-vinuni-library-account/)** on the library website.
* Sharing VinUni library ID or VinUni cards with others is strictly prohibited.

[Library Resources](https://library.vinuni.edu.vn/)

## Regulations on the use of library materials

**1. Circulation regulations for library materials**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **No.** | **Material types** | **Circulated or Uncirculated** | **Circulation locations** | **Notes** |
| 1 | General print books | Circulated | * Self-check machines (1st Floor & 2nd Floor) * Circulation desk (1st Floor) * 24/7 book return machine |  |
| 2 | Reference materials and print journals | Uncirculated | * Not applicable |  |
| 3 | Audiovisual materials (CDs/DVDs) | Circulated | * Circulation desk (1st Floor) only |  |
| 4 | Books in Course Reserve collection | Circulated | * Circulation desk (1st Floor) only | These books can be checked out for 02 hours only (01 item/user/time). |

* Patrons MUST return or renew all borrowed materials before its overdue time.
* The renewal period is half of the original loan period. Renewals of library materials are only allowed if there has been no request for that material by others. Materials that are not overdue can be renewed in person at the circulation desk, or online. Overdue materials cannot be renewed.
* Patrons need to inform library staff at the circulation desk about any damage to the materials before check-out/check-in.
* When the due date falls on a holiday or when the library is closed, the due date is adjusted to the following day when the library is open.
* Patrons are prohibited to misfile or hide library materials for personal use or bring physical materials out of the library without going through borrowing procedures as regulated.
* All users must return all loans, fines and debts must be paid, one month before their departure days.
* All mercies regarding fines and loan periods need to be approved by university senior leaders as regulated by VinUni’s document of authorization.

**2. Circulation Privileges**

|  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **User groups** | **Number of items** | **Loan Period** | **Renewals** |
| * Undergraduate students | 3 | 2 weeks | 1 time |
| * Graduate students | 5 | 1 month | 1 time |
| * Affiliated faculty * VinUni’s full time staff / Service-contract staff | 3 | 2 weeks | 1 time |
| * VinUni’s faculty | 5 | 6 months | 1 time |
| * Visiting students | To be determined by library leader. | | |
| * Guests/Interns/Alumni | 0 | 0 | 0 |

*Note: The loan period of 06 months will be applied for faculty who borrow textbooks for course use. However, their loaned items can be subject to recall if the items are requested by other users, and they need to send a request to the library about the textbooks for course use.*

**3. Making physical material request**

* Patrons can make a request via the library website to borrow a physical book or a CD/DVD, etc. that has already been circulated to others. A material request will not be placed if:
* The item is in the course reserve collection.
* The item is lost, missing, or non-circulating.
* Patrons’ borrowing privileges have been blocked or suspended.
* A patron has reached their loan limit (the maximum number of items that they can borrow).
* Library items ready for pickup will be kept for 02 days from the tentative pick-up date. If it is not picked up by the stipulated date, the request will lapse.
* Patrons themselves must personally collect the library materials on hold for them.

**4. Recalls**

All loans are subject to recall after one-week’s usage. If an item is recalled, a Recall Notice will be sent to patrons via email. Patrons are requested to return materials by a new shorter due date.

**5. Electronic Resources Use**

Subscribed electronic resources such as databases, e-journals and e-books are covered by licensing agreements between the VinUni and individual providers. Therefore,

* The use of subscribed electronic resources at the library is limited to VinUni students, faculty, and staff only, unless allowed by the license agreement.
* Patrons accessing library databases will be required to log in with their VinUni ID.
* Patrons must use electronic resources properly; ensuring that materials retrieved from electronic resources are limited to personal and non-commercial purposes. Improper use generally includes, but is not limited to:

**Excessive downloading:**

* Rapid downloading of multiple articles.
* Continuous downloading of tables of contents, search results, or citations.
* Downloading entire issues or volumes of an e-journal.
* Using robots, spiders, or other automated programs to download subscription resources

**Other license violations:**

* Sharing passwords with unauthorized patrons
* Redistributing information from online resources to unauthorized patrons
* Posting information retrieved or downloaded from an online resource to a listserv, on a website, or to an email list
* Reselling, redistributing, or reproducing any journal text, output, search result, or other information in any form or medium
* Redistribution of materials to non-subscribers or non-subscribing institutions.

Unauthorized use of VinUni Library databases and other electronic resources can result in access being revoked for the entire university, and therefore will be disciplined as outlined in Section 4 below, or by referral to appropriate civil authorities.

**6. Material photocopying/ printing/scanning services**

* Photocopying/printing/scanning services are provided for patrons with research, personal study, and non-commercial purposes.
* Patrons must avoid any breach of copyright in using photocopiers, printers, and scanners in the library including but not limited to:
  + Making more than one copy of the work by an author for any purpose without author’s permission.
  + Digitize physical materials and distribute the digital copy to other people with any purposes.
  + Modify, mutilate, or misrepresent the material in any manner which is prejudicial to the honor and reputation of the authors.
  + Create derivative material (the material, which is translated from one language into another adapted, modified, transformed, etc. from other materials) without permission of the authors/ copyright owners of original materials.
  + Intentionally rescind or invalidate technical measures taken by copyright owners to protect copyright in their materials.
  + Deliberately delete or change copyright management information in electronic format contained in the materials.
* Users are fully responsible for any legal consequences concerning copyright that might arise from using the library’s photocopiers, printers, and scanners.

# Health & Well-being

![](https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/wp-content/uploads/sites/20/2025/05/aaa.png)

Peer Support: If you’re hesitant to speak with a counsellor, consider talking to a peer supporter. These trained students, supervised by professional psychologists, are here to listen. Book an appointment with a peer supporter **[here](https://vinunipsg.simplybook.asia/v2/?bdp-pixel=1&widget-type=fbdp#book)**.  
Stay Connected: We also host events and activities to promote wellbeing. Follow us on social media to stay updated:

* Facebook: [**VinUni Wellbeing Services**](https://www.facebook.com/vinuni.wellbeing)
* Instagram: **@vinuni.peersupport**

[Book An Appointment](https://my.vinuni.edu.vn/student/s/my-services)

# Research Opportunities

### Student Research at VinUni

Undergraduate Research at VinUniversity is a core part of the training and education of VinUni students. VinUni recognizes that research skills and competencies are not just critical for those who wish to purse academic work, but for any person who wishes to be a contributor, leader and a change maker in the society. Research skills of systematic inquiry, information gathering, critical analysis, problem solving, resilience and focus, interpretation and communication, are core life skills that are part of VinUni’s mission for students!  
First year students are already engaged in independent research under the guidance of VinUni faculty members, and working with faculty on their Seed Grants and other research projects.

[CECS](https://cecs.vinuni.edu.vn/uropcecs/)

[![logo](https://experience.vinuni.edu.vn/wp-content/themes/launch-pad/assets/images/logo_vin.png)](javascript:void(0);)

Copyright © 2025 VinUni. All Right Reserved.

INTEGRATED DEGREE PROGRAMS

At VinUniversity (VinUni), we offer integrated degree programs alongside traditional tracks, allowing students to fast track their studies. These programs, established with renowned partner universities, enable students to earn two bachelor’s degrees or a bachelor’s and a master’s degree.
Students will complete Phase 1 at VinUni and Phase 2 at a partner university abroad. Program formats include 3+2, 4+1, and 2+2 options.
The fast-tracking Bachelor’s–Master’s module lets students earn credits toward their Master’s while completing their Bachelor’s, potentially saving costs and allowing graduation up to 12 months earlier. This pathway promotes a smooth transition to higher degrees efficiently and cost-effectively.

VinUniversity Career Services guide students through the R.E.D.I. Roadmap (Reflect, Explore, Develop, Implement) and 5C Model—offering career exploration, career readiness workshops, 1-on-1 career counseling, career mentoring, and career checkpoints (including Career Festival and Career Day). Through the EXCEL framework, students build key skills and gain real-world experience via work-study roles and internships with top companies. Support continues up to two years after graduation. Additional resources include job search tools, the Career Development Guidebook and the Career Advancement Guidebook. VinUniversity works closely with industry partners to bridge academics and employment.

The Student Work-Study Program at VinUniversity is designed to provide students with opportunities to gain practical work experience, develop valuable skills, and contribute to the university community. This program also aims to support students financially while fostering their professional and personal growth.

Our international students are not just part of the VinUni family, they are ambassadors of their cultures and essential contributors to our global dialogue. At VinUniversity, we celebrate this diversity daily, ensuring that every voice is heard and every perspective valued. Together, we’re not just building a university, we’re creating a global community of leaders, thinkers, and innovators.

VinUniversity provides dedicated support for international students, including visa guidance, financial advising, transportation support, housing assistance, and diverse dining options. These services ensure a smooth transition and help students feel at home while focusing on their academic and personal growth in Vietnam.

VinUniversity hosts a dynamic international student community from over 16 countries. International students receive a globally competitive education and are actively involved in campus life through co-living, student clubs like VISA, and cultural events such as the Hanoi Tour and Year-End Party.
With strong support services, including visa assistance and the Student Affairs Office, VinUniversity ensures a smooth transition and enriching university experience.

VinUniversity utilizes **Canvas**, a web-based Learning Management System (LMS), for both credit and non-credit courses. Instructors use Canvas to manage course content, assignments, quizzes, schedules, and communication, while students are expected to log in daily to track coursework. Canvas offers interactive features like threaded discussions, video conferencing, and group collaboration tools.  
Before the semester starts, all students must complete the **Passport to Canvas** online course to familiarize themselves with the platform. To enhance accessibility, students are encouraged to download the **Canvas app** on their mobile devices for seamless learning and engagement throughout their academic journey.

### 1. What is elab

Entrepreneurship Lab (E-Lab) is the heart of innovation at VinUniversity. We provide a hands-on, student-led technopreneurial ecosystem where students, alumni, and young entrepreneurs transform ideas into impactful startups through mentorship, funding, and structured programs.

[INTRODUCING VIDEO](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=4d1xDyKgqRc&t=5s)

### 2. Why Elab?

Backed by Vingroup – Vietnam’s leading conglomerate  
Global-local network: Cornell, Upenn, FUV, HUST, and more  
100+ C-level mentors and entrepreneurs-in-residence  
Startup-friendly ecosystem: co-working space, free tools, legal support  
Impact: 40+ student startups/year, $3.5M+ raised, 2,000+ community members

### 3. Key Programs and Services

**Pre-Incubator**

Focus: Ideation, early-stage development  
Activities: Peer mentoring, workshops, competitions  
Open to: All students

**Incubator**

Focus: Business model validation  
Benefits: $2,000 grant, mentorship, workspace  
Open to: Teams with at least one VinUnian

**Pre-Accelerator (Venture X)**

Focus: Scaling, investment readiness  
Benefits: $10,000 seed fund, pitch events, global mentors  
Co-hosted with HUB Network

Work-Study Program 2024-2025: Practical Work Opportunities for VinUniversity Students

### 1. Purpose

The Student Work-Study Program at VinUniversity is designed to provide students with opportunities to gain practical work experience, develop valuable skills, and contribute to the university community. This program also aims to support students financially while fostering their professional and personal growth.

### 2. Program details

### 2.1. Work Hours

Students participating in the Work-Study Program are limited to working a maximum of 24 hours per week during the academic year. This restriction ensures that students can balance their academic commitments effectively while gaining work experience and complying to Vietnamese laws.

### 2.2. Pay rates

Pay rates are established according to the skills and responsibilities specified in the job description.

The two standard pay rates are:

* 41,667 VND/hour: For roles that require basic skills or minimal prior experience.
* 83,333 VND/hour: For roles that demand advanced skills or specialized expertise, as outlined in the job description.

### 2.3. Placement Areas:

Students can be placed in various departments, including academic offices, research projects, library services, student affairs, and others, based on the specific needs of each department during the academic year.

### 2.4. Eligibility Criteria:

a) Must be a full-time undergraduate or graduate student.

b) Maintain satisfactory academic progress, as per university regulations.

c) Demonstrate financial need (if applicable) or meet department-specific requirements.

d) Commitment to balancing work responsibilities with academic obligations.

### 2.5. Application Process

**Step 1: Job postings available:**

All available Work-Study positions will be listed on the [Job Portal](http://careerportal.vinuni.edu.vn/).

**Step 2: Application period:**

Students can submit their applications through Job Portal

**Step 3: Application review:**

The Student Affairs Management (SAM) team will collect all applications and forward them to hiring supervisors for review

**Step 4: Interview & selection:**

Colleges and departments will conduct interviews with applicants and finalize the results

**Step 5: Final results & onboarding:**

SAM will send offer letters and provide contract processing details

Successful applicants may have to attend the Onboarding Orientation

Step: Start of work:

The employment contract will be effective and announced by SAM

### 3. Reference

[Student Work-Study Program Guidelines – VinUniversity Policy](https://policy.vinuni.edu.vn/all-policies/work-study-program-guidelines/)
